description: Deduplicate the character-for-character identical `MaterializeConcretePath` implementations currently in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteFlattener.cs` by moving the shared logic into a single utility in the backend path-support layer, preferably `RelationalJsonPathSupport`. Acceptance: only one production implementation of `MaterializeConcretePath` remains; both callers use the shared helper; behavior for wildcard substitution is unchanged; the earlier false-positive note about `OrdinalPathMap<T>` is not acted on because only `MaterializeConcretePath` is duplicated.
steps-to-verify: Run `rg -n "private static string MaterializeConcretePath|public static string MaterializeConcretePath|internal static string MaterializeConcretePath" src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend` and confirm there is a single shared implementation instead of one copy in each of the two original files.
Add or update unit tests that cover successful wildcard materialization plus the error cases for too few and too many ordinal components.

description: Remove the default `ReferenceExtractionMode extractionMode = ReferenceExtractionMode.RelationalWriteValidation` parameter from `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Extraction/ReferenceExtractor.cs` so every caller must state whether it wants `LegacyCompatibility` or `RelationalWriteValidation`. Acceptance: `ExtractReferences` has no defaulted mode parameter; all production callers pass the mode explicitly; all test callers are updated to be explicit instead of relying on the default; production behavior is unchanged because `UseRelationalBackend` already selects the mode in `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware`.
steps-to-verify: Run `rg -n "ExtractReferences\(" src/dms` and confirm every call site now passes an explicit `ReferenceExtractionMode` argument.
Add or update middleware-level coverage to prove `UseRelationalBackend=true` uses `RelationalWriteValidation` and `UseRelationalBackend=false` uses `LegacyCompatibility`.

description: Remove the unused 3-parameter convenience constructor on `ReferenceDerivedValueSourceMetadata` in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs`. That overload currently aliases `ReferenceJsonPath` as both `IdentityJsonPath` and `ReferenceJsonPath`, but the current branch has zero production or test callers and the shorter overload is dead code. Acceptance: the 3-parameter constructor is removed; no callers remain; all surviving construction sites use the 4-parameter form with both the authoritative target `IdentityJsonPath` and the request-facing `ReferenceJsonPath` supplied explicitly.
steps-to-verify: Run `rg -n "new ReferenceDerivedValueSourceMetadata\(" src/dms` and confirm all call sites use the explicit 4-parameter constructor shape.

description: Remove the 2-parameter convenience constructor on `ReferenceIdentityBinding` in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/RelationalModelTypes.cs` and make all call sites specify both `IdentityJsonPath` and `ReferenceJsonPath` explicitly. The short form currently aliases the two paths, which is a real Ed-Fi pattern in some tests but obscures the distinction this branch just hardened between target identity metadata and request member paths. Acceptance: the short constructor is removed; production code continues to use the explicit 3-parameter constructor; all test code is updated to pass both paths intentionally, using the same path twice only where that equality is truly intended and visible in source.
steps-to-verify: Run `rg -n "new ReferenceIdentityBinding\(" src/dms` and confirm every call site now provides both `IdentityJsonPath` and `ReferenceJsonPath` explicitly before the column argument.
Preserve or extend the hardening coverage around duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` values and distinct `IdentityJsonPath` values so the explicit metadata distinction remains tested.

description: Reconcile the divergent descriptor identity detection heuristics between `IdentityValueCanonicalizer.IsDescriptorValuedIdentityMember` in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Extraction/IdentityValueCanonicalizer.cs` and `FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet.IsDescriptorIdentityPath` in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet.cs`. The backend logic currently handles the synthetic `DocumentIdentity.DescriptorIdentityJsonPath` sentinel (`$.descriptor`) and array/concrete suffix cases such as paths ending in `Descriptor]`, while the Core canonicalizer only checks `EndsWith("Descriptor")`. Acceptance: descriptor identity classification is centralized or clearly shared so Core and Backend no longer drift silently; the chosen helper preserves the current backend behavior for synthetic descriptor identity metadata and concrete array-element paths; existing descriptor-backed reference materialization scenarios continue to pass.
steps-to-verify: Add or update tests to cover all relevant cases: the synthetic descriptor sentinel `$.descriptor`, a normal descriptor identity path such as `$.schoolTypeDescriptor`, and concrete or array-derived paths that need descriptor classification in backend matching.
